input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete setup plan for .NET MAUI on Windows 11 targeting Android + Windows:

---

## 1. Install .NET SDK (latest with MAUI support)

```powershell
# Discover latest SDK version
winget search Microsoft.DotNet.SDK

# Install (adjust version as needed)
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.8 --silent

# Verify
dotnet --list-sdks
```

## 2. Install the MAUI workload

```powershell
# List available workloads to confirm name
dotnet workload search maui

# Install MAUI (includes Android, Windows targets)
dotnet workload install maui

# Verify installed workloads
dotnet workload list
```

## 3. Install Visual Studio Build Tools (Windows target needs WinUI/Windows App SDK)

```powershell
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.Community --silent `
  --override "--add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.ManagedDesktop `
              --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.NetCrossPlat `
              --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.ComponentGroup.WindowsAppSDK.Cs `
              --includeRecommended --quiet --wait"
```

## 4. Android SDK setup

The MAUI workload installs a minimal Android SDK. To manage it explicitly:

```powershell
# Find where the Android SDK was placed
dotnet android sdk info

# Or locate it manually
$androidSdk = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk"
if (!(Test-Path $androidSdk)) {
    $androidSdk = "$env:ProgramFiles (x86)\Android\android-sdk"
}

# Use sdkmanager to discover available platforms/build-tools
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list

# Install required components (adjust API level dynamically)
$latest = & "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list |
    Select-String "platforms;android-(\d+)" |
    ForEach-Object { [int]$_.Matches.Groups[1].Value } |
    Sort-Object -Descending | Select-Object -First 1

& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" `
    "platforms;android-$latest" `
    "build-tools;$latest.0.0" `
    "platform-tools"
```

## 5. Accept Android licenses

```powershell
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --licenses
# Answer 'y' to all prompts
```

## 6. Enable Developer Mode (required for Windows target)

```powershell
# Enable Developer Mode via registry
reg add "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\AppModelUnlock" `
    /t REG_DWORD /f /v "AllowDevelopmentWithoutDevLicense" /d "1"
```

## 7. Create and build a test project

```powershell
dotnet new maui -n MauiTest
cd MauiTest

# Build for Windows
dotnet build -f net8.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net8.0-android
```

## 8. Run on Android Emulator

```powershell
# Create an AVD (discover system images first)
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\avdmanager.bat" list device
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" "system-images;android-$latest;google_apis;x86_64"
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\avdmanager.bat" create avd `
    -n "Pixel_MAUI" -k "system-images;android-$latest;google_apis;x86_64" --device "pixel_6"

# Launch emulator
Start-Process "$androidSdk\emulator\emulator.exe" -ArgumentList "-avd Pixel_MAUI"

# Deploy
dotnet build -f net8.0-android -t:Run
```

---

**Key tips:**
- Replace `net8.0` with `net9.0` if using .NET 9 (`dotnet --version` to check).
- Use `dotnet workload update` periodically to keep Android SDK components in sync.
- If Hyper-V isn't enabled (needed for fast Android emulator): `Enable-WindowsOptionalFeature -Online -FeatureName Microsoft-Hyper-V -All`.
